_meta.authored_from: Swift MediaLibraryStore.normalizedShowTitle (apple Sources/EmeraldKit/State/MediaLibraryStore.swift ~L66) + its inline vector pin Tests/EmeraldKitTests/NormalizedShowTitleVectorTests.swift (apple 09dd5fe)
_meta.author: ChrisPachulski
_meta.purpose: Cross-language pin for the last-resort show-title join key ('The American Experiment' incident). The Swift MediaLibraryStore.normalizedShowTitle and Rust media_core::filename::normalize_show_name compute the SAME join key independently and were coupled only by a doc comment; the trailing-year strip was fixed independently in BOTH on the same day (apple 6368b8d, server dd7a817) because nothing pinned it. This file freezes the input->key contract so a unilateral tweak on either side goes red before it can silently desync the join.
_meta.known_drift: The Swift and Rust normalizers are NOT byte-identical today. Swift additionally (a) ASCII-folds diacritics, (b) maps '&' -> 'and', (c) splits on ALL non-alphanumerics (so an apostrophe is a token separator), and (d) guards the trailing-year strip with `parts.count > 1` so a title that is ONLY a year is KEPT. Rust's clean() treats only '.', '_', '-' and whitespace as separators, does not fold diacritics, does not expand '&', AND its strip_trailing_year (filename.rs ~L144) has NO lone-token guard, so normalize_show_name('1923') strips the only token and returns "" (empty) rather than '1923'. That last divergence is join-CRITICAL: an id-less show literally named for a year (e.g. Paramount's '1923') normalizes to empty on the Rust side and loses its last-resort Play-button join — the exact hazard the Swift doc comment calls out and guards against. Vectors that exercise these divergences carry "swift_only": true and "rust_expected" with the value the CURRENT Rust normalizer produces, so the Rust suite pins actual behaviour instead of a red aspiration. Consumers that require true cross-language parity must reconcile the two normalizers; until then these are documented drift, not test failures.

equivalence_classes.name: pokemon-spellings
equivalence_classes.rust_note: Rust does not diacritic-fold, so 'Pokémon' does not join 'pokemon' under normalize_show_name.

equivalence_classes.name: will-and-grace-spellings
equivalence_classes.rust_note: Rust does not expand '&' to 'and', so the '&' spellings do not join 'Will and Grace' under normalize_show_name.
